title: Unable to pull images on private container registry.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
When I try to pull an image on a private container registry with a worker node, I get the following error message and cannot pull the image.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.Pull the private container registry image (registry:2.8.0) and start it with the private container registry server (podman v4.0.2).
https://hub.docker.com/layers/library/registry/2.8.0/images/sha256-712c58f0d738ba95788d2814979028fd648a37186ae0dd4141f786125ba6d680?context=explore

2.Register images with podman load/tag/push

3.Pull image on worker node

**Describe the results you received:**
```
[root@worker1 containers]# podman --log-level debug pull container-registry:5050/test/ap-wls-image:AA_BB-20220824-1
:
:
DEBU[0033] Error pulling candidate container-registry:5050/test/ap-wls-image:AA_BB-20220824-1: trying to reuse blob sha256:3966bb1d13ea386db38bc89f0d5cb027a00030f4c69b4e86345368762f6b425c at destination: adding layer with blob "sha256:92e1790889cad2cfa31a94145181252bb9f250f156e647bf08bcc28b61f8d5af": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): Error after fallback to chroot: no such file or directory 
Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:3966bb1d13ea386db38bc89f0d5cb027a00030f4c69b4e86345368762f6b425c at destination: adding layer with blob "sha256:92e1790889cad2cfa31a94145181252bb9f250f156e647bf08bcc28b61f8d5af": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): Error after fallback to chroot: no such file or directory
[root@worker1 containers]# 
```
**Describe the results you expected:**
```
[root@worker1 containers]# podman --log-level debug pull container-registry:5050/test/ap-wls-image:AA_BB-20220824-1
:
:
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@worker1 containers]# 
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
The problem occurs almost every time, but occasionally the image pull succeeds and the container is started.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
"worker node"
[root@worker1 ~]# podman version 
Version:      3.3.1
API Version:  3.3.1
Go Version:   go1.16.7
Built:        Thu Nov 11 00:32:15 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@worker1 ~]# 
```
```
"private container registry"
[root@container-registry ~]# podman version 
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:        Tue May 17 10:00:36 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
[root@worker1 ~]# 
```

No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Virtual machine virtualized by OLVM

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @miyao-kota.

Do you get this error for all images? Can you try with a newer version of Podman?

@vrothberg 
Thanks for the reply. 
I am having an issue with some images not being able to pull, not all images.
Since podman is installed as a dependency of a package called OCNE(Oracle Cloud Native Environment), I will check the impact on OCNE before upgrading.
Sorry for this, but I am unable to upgrade immediately.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing this, since I think this is fixed in latest podman.
